title: Pune realtor murder: Siya 'secretly' married Chetan months before killing Ketan
content: Police are investigating claims that Siya Goyal secretly married Chetan Chaudhary four months prior to the murder. This alleged marriage occurred after Goyal's engagement to businessman Ketan Agarwal. Investigators are verifying marriage registration and examining digital evidence for proof. Deleted photographs and chat messages are crucial elements in the ongoing probe.

title: Seconds before disaster: CCTV shows Wayanad landslide sweeping away fuel tanker
content: A landslide near Wayanad's tunnel project site caused significant destruction and panic.  Two people died and seven others sustained injuries in the sudden debris flow.  Several individuals remain missing after the devastating incident occurred near Meenakshi Bridge.  Heavy rainfall and construction debris are under scrutiny as contributing factors.  Rescue operations are ongoing to locate the missing and clear the affected area.

title: Former Afghanistan fast bowler Shapoor Zadran passes away after prolonged illness
content: Former Afghanistan fast bowler Shapoor Zadran died in India on Tuesday. He was 38 and had been battling a rare illness for months. Zadran was a pioneer in Afghanistan's cricket journey, playing 80 international matches. He helped the team qualify for major global tournaments during his career. His passing has prompted widespread tributes from the cricket community.

title: As Microsoft announces 4800 layoffs, Amy Coleman makes a commitment to all employees
content: Microsoft is laying off 4,800 employees, about 2.1% of its global workforce, as part of a major restructuring across its commercial sales and Xbox divisions. HR head Amy Coleman shared the news in a memo to employees, saying the company is "always looking for ways to reduce the need for job eliminations." Read her complete memo detailing the reasons behind Microsoft's latest round of layoffs.

title: 'India strong advocate of a free Indo-Pacific': PM Modi's strong message to China
content: Prime Minister Modi addressed the Indonesian Parliament, highlighting shared civilizational ties. He called for closer cooperation based on democratic values and development. Modi received Indonesia's highest civilian honor, the Bintang Adipurna. He dedicated this award to the people of India and the partnership. The Prime Minister emphasized India's developmental approach over expansionism.

title: The biggest hurdle in India's chip race isn't the plan—it's execution
content: India's semiconductor strategy draws on proven Asian models for growth.  Execution, not strategy, presents the nation's primary challenge now.  Developing skilled workers and strengthening local supply chains are crucial steps.  The country focuses on outsourced assembly and mature process nodes.  Meeting global quality standards and increasing domestic production are key goals.

title: 'My visit continues': Macron after close shave in Syria amid explosions near hotel
content: French President Emmanuel Macron continued his Syria visit after explosions near his hotel. Two blasts struck central Damascus, wounding at least eighteen people earlier on Tuesday. Macron was unharmed and proceeded with his scheduled meeting with Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa. Further details indicated two explosive devices detonated near the Four Seasons Hotel. No group has claimed responsibility for the recent attack in Damascus.

title: Jamie Dimon gives a 'reality check' to Canada's PM Mark Carney on his unity call to Europe
content: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon has dismissed Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney's call for middle powers to unite, calling it a "fantasy." Dimon pointed to Europe's economic struggles as an example of cooperation's limitations, citing high taxes and regulation. He emphasized that attractive business environments are crucial for growth, contrasting Europe's declining competitiveness with the U.S. market. Dimon also reassured investors about America's investment appeal, noting China's evolving global approach.

title: WC: Blow for England before Norway clash, star player ruled out after freak injury
content: England midfielder Jordan Henderson suffered a serious wrist injury while celebrating a World Cup victory.  He sustained the injury after slipping while attempting to celebrate with fans.  Henderson was taken to a hospital in Mexico City for treatment.  The team advanced to the quarter-finals despite this unfortunate setback.  England will now face Norway in their next tournament match.

title: World's largest asset management firm CEO worried about Mamdani running New York
content: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink voiced concerns about New York City's business climate. He noted that 47% of city tax revenue comes from the top 1% of taxpayers. Fink stated that inadequate services do not justify the current tax burden for residents. He believes economic growth should be prioritized over tax policy by policymakers. Fink suggested that BlackRock might deploy US resources elsewhere if conditions weaken.

title: 'Gaddar', 'beimaan' chants greet TMC rebel Saayoni Ghosh during her Baruipur visit
content: MP Saayoni Ghosh encountered hostile protests and mocking chants during her visit to Baruipur. The crowd targeted her recent defection from the TMC, branding her a turncoat. Police initially blocked the delegation but later allowed them entry amid security. Ghosh emphasized the need for swift justice for the victim and her family. Various political leaders also met the victim's parents to offer support.

title: Video captures terrifying moment of landslide hitting Wayanad; 2 dead, 7 injured
content: A significant landslide occurred in Wayanad, Kerala, near a tunnel construction site. Local residents rescued three individuals from the debris after the incident. Five workers sustained injuries and are receiving medical attention at a hospital. Rescue operations are underway with police and NDRF teams deployed. Officials are coordinating efforts to ascertain if more people are trapped.

title: Flights, hotels, visa in one go: 10 things to know about Saudi’s Package Visa
content: Saudi Arabia has introduced a new pilot Package Visa service for tourists. This digital initiative integrates flight and accommodation bookings with visa applications. Eligible travelers can now book their entire trip through a single, unified travel package. The service aims to simplify the visitor journey and encourage longer stays. This move supports Saudi Arabia's growing tourism sector and global accessibility.

title: Blake Lively, Ryan Reynolds reportedly devastated over Swift-Kelce wedding snub
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce just had the wedding of the year as billions across the world cheered them on throughout the world across countries. While the wedding pictures have been kept strictly private, Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds remained missing from the party. At a time when Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce publicly distanced themselves from the Hollywood power couple, it seems both Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds are furious at this.

title: Watch: Explosions near Macron's Damascus hotel as French leader meets Syria's president
content: Explosions struck central Damascus near President Emmanuel Macron's hotel. The French president proceeded with his scheduled meeting with Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa. Macron is the first EU leader to visit Syria since 2024. His visit focuses on reconstruction and restoring diplomatic engagement. Discussions also covered counterterrorism cooperation and regional security matters.

title: Who is Erling Haaland's sister? Gabrielle goes viral at FIFA World Cup 2026
content: Erling Haaland has become one of the biggest stories of the FIFA World Cup 2026. While the Norway striker has impressed on the pitch, one of his family members has unexpectedly grabbed attention off it. His older sister, Gabrielle Braut Haaland, has gone viral after cameras spotted her cheering from the stands.

title: 'I have not made such statement': Suryakumar breaks silence on viral fake quote
content: Suryakumar Yadav has denied a fake statement circulating on social media regarding his captaincy. He expressed happiness for the team and affirmed his unwavering support for Indian cricket. The selectors continue their youth-first approach, naming Shreyas Iyer as captain for the Zimbabwe series too. Suryakumar also congratulated Vaibhav Sooryavanshi on becoming India's youngest international cricketer.

title: What sparked Kylian Mbappe-Celeste Amarilla World Cup controversy?
content: France’s World Cup win over Paraguay was overshadowed by a racism controversy involving Paraguayan senator Celeste Amarilla. After a tense post-match incident between Kylian Mbappe and Orlando Gill, Amarilla posted offensive remarks about the France star on X. Mbappe responded publicly, condemning the comments and defending Paraguay’s players while calling out racism and hatred.

title: 'Not his fault': Belgium coach Garcia defends Balogun after FIFA controversy
content: Belgium's coach Rudi Garcia expressed understanding regarding Folarin Balogun's eligibility situation.  He stated the player was not to blame for the FIFA eligibility controversy.  Belgium secured a dominant 4-1 victory against the United States in their match.  Charles De Ketelaere scored twice, leading the team's strong offensive performance.  The team now advances to face Spain in the quarter-finals.

title: Suspecting wife's fidelity, Karnataka man kicks infant, throws baby to floor during fight
content: A father has been arrested in Bengaluru for allegedly killing his infant daughter. The child died after her father reportedly kicked and threw her during an argument. Initially, the parents claimed the baby had fallen accidentally from a cot. Post-mortem examination revealed severe internal injuries inconsistent with a fall. Police are now searching for the mother who is currently absconding.

title: Hema Malini reveals Dharmendra's last wish: 'Keep the family united' before his passing
content: Hema Malini spoke about Dharmendra's final message to keep the family united.  She mentioned that Dharmendra always emphasized spending time with children and family.  Malini described Dharmendra's sons, Sunny and Bobby Deol, as wonderful boys.  She stated that they are all internally united and a happy family.  Dharmendra was a generous and loving person who taught facing life's difficulties.

title: Taj Mahal originally temple? HC seeks response of Centre, ASI on inspection plea
content: The Allahabad High Court has directed the central government and ASI to file responses. This follows a petition challenging an Agra court's refusal to allow a Taj Mahal survey. Petitioners claim the monument is an ancient Hindu temple called Tejo Mahalaya. They seek permission to perform prayers and conduct a survey. The court is asked to set aside lower court orders and decide the application.

title: Did illegal immigration drive up US home prices? Here's what a Fed study found
content: A federal reserve bank of Dallas report linked rising unauthorized immigration to increased housing demand. This surge between 2021 and 2024 significantly boosted home prices and rents. The research indicated unauthorized immigrants contributed substantially to employment and housing cost growth. Residential construction lagged behind the rapid increase in housing needs. Other economic factors also influenced the US housing market during this period.

title: Abhishek Sharma's AI image case: HC flags 'thin line' between personality rights & defamation
content: The Delhi High Court is examining personality rights in the digital age.  Cricketer Abhishek Sharma filed a suit against unauthorised online use of his likeness.  The court noted a thin line between defamation and personality rights.  Disputes involve AI-altered images and the exploitation of identity.  The matter was adjourned for further hearing on July 9.

title: Ram Temple theft row: UP CM Yogi calls out Congress, SP with 'chameleon' dig
content: A day after the Ram Mandir Trust accepted the resignation of Champat Rai from the position of general secretary, Yogi accused the rival political parties, Congress and the Samajwadi Party, of "making a mockery of Hindu faith" -- the same allegation the opposition has been levelling against the BJP.  Yogi futher asked why the anti-NDA bloc remained muted when hectares of land across the nation were "sold off in the name of the Wakf".

title: Zoho co-founder Sridhar Vembu's veshti moment on Japan's bullet train goes viral
content: Sridhar Vembu's photo on Japan's bullet train wearing a veshti went viral.  This image resonated with many as a natural display of culture.  Vembu leads Zoho's global business from a Tamil Nadu village.  He aims to foster rural Indian and Japanese business connections.  The photograph highlighted cultural pride and personal authenticity.

title: AB de Villiers, Virat Kohli, Jasprit Bumrah are rare: Wiaan Mulder
content: South Africa all-rounder Wiaan Mulder believes the future belongs to specialists, saying the physical and technical demands of modern cricket make it increasingly difficult to excel across all three formats. In an exclusive interaction with TimesofIndia. com, the South African all-rounder discusses the evolution of T20 cricket, his reservations about the Impact Player rule, cricket's growth in the USA, and why SA20 has played a key role in the Proteas' recent success.

title: India's strategic gas storage: LNG buffer mandate for terminals in the works
content: The disruption to LNG shipments through the Strait of Hormuz during the Iran conflict highlighted India's exposure to supply disruptions, prompting the government to revisit the idea of creating strategic gas reserves. Policymakers are evaluating a plan under which LNG terminal operators would be required to expand storage capacity at their existing import facilities.

title: Want a stake in Cochin Shipyard? Centre launches offer for sale at Rs 1,400 per share
content: The Centre is selling a 2.52% stake in Cochin Shipyard Limited through an Offer for Sale. Non-retail investors can bid on July 7, 2026, and retail investors on July 8, 2026. This divestment supports the government's extensive maritime ecosystem expansion programs. Initiatives like the Maritime Development Fund and financial assistance schemes are bolstering the sector. The long-term Maritime Amrit Kaal Vision 2047 aims for significant growth by 2047.

title: 'You can complain, nothing will happen': Who protects you when your ride goes wrong?
content: Passengers face challenges with ride-hailing app complaint systems.  Many incidents involve AI chatbots and a lack of human support.  Commuters report issues like driver misbehavior and privacy invasions.  App aggregators claim a two-tier system handles serious safety concerns.  New government initiatives aim to enhance passenger safety during rides.

title: FIFA WC: Belgium players mimic Donald Trump's dance after win over USA | Watch
content: After a convincing victory, Belgium players couldn't resist poking fun at Donald Trump's infamous dance moves. Romelu Lukaku and his teammates celebrated in style, with a social media post cheekily referencing a FIFA ruling that permitted Folarin Balogun to take the field post red card. Belgium has now confidently moved on to the quarter-finals after their stunning defeat of the USA.

title: Donald Trump may allow Turkey back into F-35 fighter jet programme: Report
content: US President Trump may allow Turkey back into the F-35 fighter jet program. This potential breakthrough follows Turkey's removal after buying Russian air defense systems. Israel's Prime Minister opposes this move, citing regional stability concerns. The development occurs as NATO leaders gather for a summit in Ankara. Defense spending and alliance security will dominate the important NATO discussions.

title: Ronaldo's World Cup ends with painful knockout record: Just one goal in 10 matches
content: Cristiano Ronaldo's FIFA World Cup journey concluded after Portugal's loss to Spain. He confirmed that the 2026 tournament would be his final appearance. Ronaldo leaves behind numerous records and international silverware for Portugal. His World Cup knockout round statistics showed only one goal and no assists. The legendary forward expressed sadness but also a clear conscience about his career.

title: Penalty for late admission for PUC students abolished in Karnataka
content: In 2018-19, the government of Karnataka had imposed a penalty of ₹670 to ₹2,220 on students who enrol late in PU colleges

title: Stake sale deal in Vizhinjam port detrimental to public interest, says Kerala Leader of the Opposition Pinarayi Vijayan
content: The former Chief Minister requests the Securities and Exchange Board of India to examine whether the disclosure made by Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Limited in relation to the share purchase and subscription agreement concerning Adani Vizhinjam Port Private Limited conforms to contractual and regulatory requirements

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE: IMD issues red, orange alerts as rain lashes Delhi; Wayanad on red alert after landslip
content: The alert was issued at 12.30 p.m. after the landslip at the Kalladi tunnel project site in Wayanad at around 11 a.m. Meanwhile, officials said ten people had died in rain-related incidents across Maharashtra's Palghar since July 1

title: Wayanad debris slip LIVE updates: Eight feared dead, one officially confirmed dead; region braces for more rains
content: ​The debris slip incident occurred near the Meenakshi Bridge at Kalladi; officials said rescue operations are underway; CM Satheesan says seven people have been hospitalised following the incident

title: Baruipur rape-murder case: West Bengal Police intensify probe; examine shack as possible crime scene
content: “Three arrested accused were being questioned even as investigators were awaiting the forensic and viscera examination reports,” says senior police officer

title: Traffic bottleneck outside Sarath City Capital Mall leaves motorists stranded for hours on Gachibowli–Miyapur corridor; underpass, skywalk pitched
content: With up to 2.5 lakh weekend visitors, the mall frontage has become one of Hyderabad’s worst traffic hotspots as vehicles on six-lane squeeze into two-lane road. Inadequate pick-up and drop-off space cripple movement.

title: Debris slip at Wayanad tunnel road project site; eight feared dead, seven missing
content: No fatalities have been officially confirmed, but authorities fear that more workers or heavy machinery operators may still be trapped beneath the collapsed wall and debris; major rescue operation on

title: IMD issues red alert for Wayanad, Kozhikode districts in Kerala warning of extreme heavy rain on Tuesday
content: Around 265 mm rain lashed the Wayanad region in the 24 hours ending at 8.30 a.m. on Tuesday (July 7, 2026), which is classified as extremely heavy rainfall by India Meteorological Department

title: All efforts on to rescue those trapped in Wayanad debris slip, says Priyanka Gandhi
content: The Wayanad MP appeals to United Democratic Front workers, functionaries and members of the public to extend all possible support

title: Three officials shunted from Environment Minister’s office in one day
content: The reasons for the action were not stated in the orders and remained unknown; a senior Environment Ministry official called the move “unprecedented”

title: PM in Indonesia LIVE: Modi hails new 'golden chapter in India and Indonesia bilateral relations'
content: PM Modi conferred with Indonesia's highest honour ’Bintang Adipurna of the Republic of Indonesia’ medal

title: Why Pakistan-based Hafiz Saeed is wanted for the Pahalgam carnage? | Explained
content: Why is Saeed so important for India? What charges is Saeed facing? Where is the LeT chief operating from?

title: 8 wildlife sanctuaries, 281 reserve forests under encroachment in Assam
content: Forest Minister Jayanta Mallabaruah tells the Assembly that more than 25,588 hectares of about 315,997 hectares under encroachment as on February 12, 2016, have been cleared

title: Karur stampede: TVK chief Vijay speaks to victims’ families on video calls
content: A core team of TVK functionaries has been facilitating the interactions through video calls, connecting Mr. Vijay. The cadres have requested the families not to record or photograph the calls.

title: 2008 Ahmedabad serial blasts: High Court upholds death penalty for 38; government calls verdict a historic victory
content: On July 26, 2008, a series of 21 bomb blasts ripped through Ahmedabad in a span of 70 minutes, killing 56 persons and injuring 200 others

title: Cops say Bengaluru daycare 'whistleblower' tortured kids too, so was arrested
content: A whistleblower nanny was arrested in Bengaluru's daycare torture case.  Sujatha, who recorded alleged toddler mistreatment, now faces charges.  Police state her motive was to implicate other staff members.  She allegedly locked a child in a bathroom, similar to another nanny.  The arrest prompts concerns about safety nets for whistleblowers.

title: Ayodhya cops eye narco, polygraph tests in Ram Temple donation theft probe
content: Ayodhya Police are considering narco-analysis and polygraph tests for five jailed accused. These examinations aim to unravel a larger conspiracy and establish the money trail. The police secured court permission to interrogate the accused inside the district jail. Investigators believe these tests will resolve statement contradictions and trace siphoned funds. The probe also examines institutional lapses and senior functionaries' potential involvement in the embezzlement.

title: Modi to inaugurate Bhogapuram airport by July-end, says  Rammohan Naidu
content: The Union Civil Aviation Minister said the ₹4,500-crore airport, built by the GMR Group on 2,200 acres, is ready for operations; air travellers’ bodies are pressing for a cut in its user fee

title: Online registration of select documents to be launched in Tamil Nadu on August 17: Minister
content: The documents include first sale of plots and flats, sale deeds relating to the Tamil Nadu Housing Board, and memoranda of deposit of title deeds (MODs).

title: Tungabhadra dam gets new crest gates, but weak monsoon threatens paddy crop, survival of rice mills in Karnataka
content: A weak monsoon has left the Tungabhadra reservoir nearly empty, threatening not only this year’s paddy crop but also an entire economy built around premium Sona Masuri rice

title: Caste census: methodology, challenges, and the road ahead | Explained
content: After decades of debate, caste will be counted as part of the 2027 Census. From the ongoing pre-test and questionnaire design to past anomalies and the road ahead, here’s what you need to know

title: In Karur, where there was no way out
content: On September 27, thousands of people gathered in Karur, Tamil Nadu, to attend a rally by actor-turned-politician Vijay. As the chief of the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam party arrived hours late, the crowd swelled and surged towards his vehicle, triggering a stampede that killed 41 people and injured over 100. C. Jaisankar and Nacchinarkkiniyan M. report on the tragedy, caused by many factors, including poor planning and alleged security lapses

title: Supreme Court declines plea to restrain TVK leaders’ remarks on Karur stampede
content: SC says it is not a political forum Court says alleged false statements should be countered with “more speech”, not judicial curbs; DMK leader R.S. Bharathi withdraws impleadment application

title: India, Indonesia expand defence ties; New Delhi to supply BrahMos missiles
content: The BrahMos missile deal with Indonesia came after India inked similar pacts with Vietnam and the Philippines

title: Safe Footpath Campaign runs into conflict with street vendors in Bengaluru
content: Hundreds of street vendors have been evicted and the wares they were hawking seized, as part of the drive in the past one week.

title: Day 16 of CJP protest: Sonam Wangchuk loses six kg as fast enters eighth day
content: Samyukta Kisan Morcha (SKM), which had earlier extended support to the CJP agitation, is now scheduled to send a delegation to the Jantar Mantar on July 7

title: Cockroach Janata Party’s X handle withheld, founder starts new account
content: The movement became an internet sensation, garnering over 14.5 million followers on Instagram, surpassing BJP’s 8.8 million follower count

title: Wayanad landslides: How two villages vanished overnight
content: Chooralmala and Mundakkai, both in Wayanad’s Meppadi panchayat, are nearly 50km away from the district headquarters in Kalpetta. Both places were known for their scenic landscapes and waterfalls.

content: The laid off staff are pinning their hopes on the talks scheduled between government officials and management representatives in Kochi on July 10

title: Blurgs AI secures $2.2 million to expand portfolio, global presence
content: Blurgs AI’s platforms are used by mission-critical organisations across defence, international maritime and conservation sectors, and India’s public sector ecosystem including the Indian Navy, the Indian Coast Guard, Bharat Electronics, DRDO Labs.

title: A different approach to the caste census | Explained
content: What have the Bihar caste survey and Telangana’s 2025 caste survey revealed? How is a social management approach different from top-down welfare models? Would a caste census deepen social divide and undermine national unity? What about democratic accountability?

title: Vietnam BrahMos deal already signed, Indonesia pact in final stages: Defence Secretary R.K. Singh
content: Indonesia said in March that it had entered into an agreement with India to procure the BrahMos missile system

title: Indian kitchens are changing. But are women still doing most of the work?
content: Men are spending more time in kitchens than ever before. But women still shoulder most of the work. We speak to people across the country about gender, labour, and who gets credit.

title: Will assess rain deficiency, drought across Karnataka: Chief Minister D. K. Shivakumar
content: The Chief Minister said his visit to Kalyana Karnataka was aimed at obtaining first-hand assessment of the prevailing conditions

title: India in talks to sell BrahMos missile, Akashteer air defence system to the UAE, sources say
content: The UAE’s interest signals a shift beyond U.S arms suppliers; sources say early stage talks are progressing quickly

title: Strict action against officials negligent in drought management, warns Karnataka Chief Minister
content: Mr. Shivakumar said the government’s foremost objective is to deliver transparent, people-centric and inclusive governance while ensuring that even the last person in society receives their constitutional and civic entitlements

title: PM Narendra Modi receives grand ceremonial welcome in Jakarta
content: The Prime Minister had arrived in Indonesia on July 6, launching the opening segment of his three-nation diplomatic visit with a high-profile ceremonial welcome that featured an escort by an Indonesian Air Force fighter jet and a personal reception by the Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto at the airport

title: CorroHealth’s terminated employees locked out of office, despite assurance
content: A meeting between Labour Minister Bindhu Krishna and top executives of the firm has been scheduled for July 10

title: Ayodhya Police to seek custody of three more in Ram Temple donation embezzlement case
content: The inquiry examined CCTV footage, statements of trust officials, bank officers, security personnel and counting staff, along with bank records, seizure documents, the MoU and Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).

title: New India Foundation announces translation fellowships
content: The selected persons are Jayasree Kalathil and Mini Chandran in Malayalam, Murali Ranganathan in Hindi, and Shefali Jha in Urdu

title: Wayanad mudslide: CM Satheesan holds emergency meeting; Agriculture Minister terms it man-made disaster
content: Agriculture Minister T. Siddique said the accident was caused by unscientifically stacked excavated soil, and alleged that the contractor’s failure to remove the accumulated soil on time led to the incident

title: Chennithala orders review of 23 child deaths in Palakkad between 2010 and 2023
content: The Home Minister’s Office said the government has tasked Deputy Inspector General of Police, Thrissur Range, T Narayanan, to head the review

title: Micro sculptures of India’s legendary scientists carved inside needle eye, set for space travel aboard Vikram-1
content: The sculptures were crafted using space-compatible materials, including stainless steel, 24-karat gold, pure silver, ceramic powder and carbon fibre particles, says Telangana’s Warangal-based international micro sculptor Ajay Kumar Mattewada

title: NSCN-IM denies role in Manipur attack, says committed to peace process
content: NSCN-IM condemns all actions detrimental to the peace process and the political talks, urging stakeholders to exercise restraint and responsibility

title: How El Niño could damage India’s economy | Explained
content: A poor monsoon can damage the economy in three ways: it affects agricultural output, reducing the sector’s contribution to the economy; it hits rural income, denting aggregate demand; and it threatens to push up food prices, causing inflation.

title: Neighbours’ quarrel over staircase leads to assault, two arrested in Anantapur
content: The two families had quarrelled over sweeping on a shared staircase; the police said the clash was a personal dispute, with no political motive

title: Tamil Nadu government inks ₹1,000-crore MoU with Hitachi for expansion projects in Porur, Chengalpattu
content: The two expansion projects that will create 1,000 high-skilled technology jobs is to be implemented over the next three to five years, said a press statement.

title: Kodagu: Two Andhra students rescued after being stranded at Mallalli falls
content: The rescued students, both 23 years old, are final-year B.Tech students from Anantapur district in Andhra Pradesh

content: A silver 'charan paduka' and silver 'Kakbhushundi' artefact, which some social media posts had claimed were among the missing items or unaccounted for, were also displayed before media personnel

title: Tamil Nadu Governor Arlekar, CM Vijay pay tributes to Rettamalai Srinivasan on birth anniversary
content: In a post on X, Mr. Vijay said Rettamalai Srinivasan was a pioneer of social justice and a powerful voice for the rights of the oppressed.

title: El Nino may reduce cyclones in Bay of Bengal
content: Tropical cyclones are among the most destructive weather systems affecting countries around the Bay of Bengal, often causing widespread damage through strong winds, heavy rainfall and storm surges

title: Fisherfolk, environmentalists demand cancellation of public hearing on Cuddalore port expansion
content: The proposed port expansion could displace fish stocks, forcing traditional fishers to venture deeper and incur higher costs, feels K.S. Gajendran of Tamil Nadu Meenavar Peravai.

title: Drunken brawl leads to chopping down of fully grown peepal and jamun trees in Hyderabad
content: The trees in question — a 40-45 years old peepal,  and a 20-25 years old jamun — were in the middle of 40-feet road in RTC Colony, Chintalkunta in the Malkajgiri Municipal Corporation’s purview

title: PM Modi conferred with Indonesia's highest honour 'Bintang Adipurna' in Jakarta
content: External Affairs Ministry spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said that the award is a "tribute to the India-Indonesia friendship."

content: The landslide occurred near Meenakshi Bridge at Kalladi, close to the site of the ongoing Anakkompoyil-Meppadi tunnel road project connecting Malappuram and Wayanad districts.

content: The woman alleged that she was held captive in a room for three days and subjected to continuous abuse at her in-laws' place.

content: Sensex Today, Nifty 50, Stock Market Highlights: Brent crude futures gained 28 cents, or 0.39%, to $72.29

title: BJP Youth Leader Detained Over Alleged Road Rage Assault In Guwahati
content: A BJP youth leader has been detained in connection with an alleged road rage assault in Assam's Guwahati, police sources said.

title: Alcoholic Telangana Man Crushes Sleeping Son's Head With Stone For Opposing House Sale
content: The accused, Kothapalli Seetharamulu alias John Mark, was arrested on Monday in connection with the murder of his son, Kiran Kumar, who is survived by his wife, Vijaya Lakshmi, and their two children.

content: Videos from the country showed mourners, including clerics wearing turbans, paying their respects at the coffins of Khamenei and four relatives killed alongside him, including a granddaughter reportedly only 14 months old.

content: Many commentators view the current debate as another chapter in Donald Trump's political story. That explanation is incomplete.

content: According to investigators, Appala Naidu's wife, Hyma, was allegedly involved in an extramarital relationship with a man identified as Gangadhar.

content: The report mentions that easing geopolitical tensions in Middle East have allowed Indian carriers to gradually restore international flight operations that had been curtailed earlier this year.

content: Mumbai, Delhi-NCR Monsoon Alert Live Updates: Special teams have been stationed at various vulnerable locations to provide immediate responses to emergencies, including tree collapses, short circuits, and partial or complete cave-ins of dilapidated buildings.

title: Gunmen Kill 9 Cops At Dam Project Checkpost In Pakistan's Balochistan
content: Militants in Balochistan ambushed a security post at the Mangi Dam, killing nine officers as region-wide infrastructure attacks intensify.

content: Heavy monsoon rains triggered deadly landslides across Rohingya refugee camps in Cox's Bazar, killing eight people, including five children

title: Australia's Under-16 Social Media Ban Fails To Clear 1st Hurdle In Age Checks
content: A new study found major platforms rarely verify users' ages, allowing under-16s to bypass Australia's landmark social media restrictions simply by entering a false birthdate.

title: Qatari LNG Carrier Hit Near Hormuz, Raising Fears Over Gulf Shipping Security
content: A projectile strike on a Qatari LNG tanker near the Strait of Hormuz has reignited concerns over maritime safety, energy supplies, and the fragile US-Iran peace process.

title: Spain hand Ronaldo brutal reality check as Belgium cancel trump card
content: Nirmalya Dutta is an editorial consultant with The Times of India. He covers world news, pop culture, football, and philosophical memes. He writes the columns Random Musings, Down the Rabbit Hole, and Offside for TOI. He also expresses his angst in The Weekly Vine, a newsletter that blends news, culture, and humour with a touch of chaos. He is the co-creator of Meow Times, a satirical cartoon strip about the absurdities of corporate life. His political, moral, and economic views drift somewhere between woke-Leninist, Rand-Marxist, and Keynesian-Friedmanite, though he isn’t entirely sure what any of those terms mean. Read More

title: US eyes tighter H-1B, green card, student visa rules; Indians could be most affected
content: Lubna Kably is a senior editor, who focuses on various policies and legislation. In particular, she writes extensively on immigration and tax policies. The Indian diaspora is the largest in the world; through her articles she demystifies the immigration-policy related developments in select countries for outbound students, job aspirants and employees. She also analyses the impact of Income-tax and GST related developments for individuals and business entities. Read More

title: SC row fallout: From ‘corruption’ to PILs — NCERT rewrites judiciary lesson
content: Manash Pratim Gohain is a seasoned journalist with over two decades at The Times of India, where he has built a rich body of work spanning education policy, politics, and governance. Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. Through his 2,500 articles and related outlets, he has emerged as a trusted voice in national discourse, particularly in linking education reform to broader societal change. Read More
